story.iWant: persist domain events to a transactional outbox in the same transaction as aggregate writes
story.soThat: downstream consumers can rely on an ordered, durable event log with at-least-once delivery and idempotent processing

tasks.task: Define OutboxEvent envelope (fields incl. schema_version, JSON schema, compat tests)
Emit representative events (TicketCreated, TicketAssigned, IssueCreated, IssueStateChanged)
Enforce uniqueness and idempotency; handle duplicates gracefully
Add unit and integration tests (commit/rollback semantics, unique constraint)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Outbox event envelope defined and serialization validated (stable fields: aggregate_id, aggregate_type, event_type, version, occurred_at, schema_version, payload)
Publisher writes outbox rows in the same transaction as domain writes; rollback leaves no rows; commit persists exactly one row
Uniqueness on (aggregate_id, version) enforced with tests; duplicates handled without duplicate side effects
Minimum event set emitted: TicketCreated, TicketAssigned, IssueCreated, IssueStateChanged with correct versioning
Micrometer metrics and structured logging implemented for publish outcomes and latency
Documentation updated for write-path, envelope, and consumer expectations

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.section: Transactional Outbox Pattern; Eventing Module; Read Models

doc.title: Eventing & Timers
doc.section: Outbox Schema & Semantics; Stream Topology; Idempotency & Versioning

doc.title: Module Boundaries
doc.section: Eventing module contracts and allowed dependencies

constraints.constraint: Outbox writes MUST occur within the same DB transaction and datasource as domain writes.
(aggregate_id, version) MUST be unique; downstream consumers MUST drop stale events with version less or equal to current.
System guarantees at-least-once delivery; publisher never deletes outbox rows; consumers mark processed_at.
Micrometer metrics and structured logs are required for publish success/error and latency.

interface.name: EventPublisher
interface.signature: publish(aggregateId: UUID, aggregateType: String, eventType: String, version: long, occurredAt: Instant, schemaVersion: int, payload: JsonNode)
